---
title: "Geospatial for Engineers"
subtitle: >-
  Earth observation and geospatial data, explained for experienced software
  engineers with zero domain background.
---

## Who this is for

You already write software. You are comfortable with JSON, the command line,
data structures, and reading an unfamiliar API. What you *don't* have is a
geospatial or earth-observation (EO) background: terms like CRS, projection,
raster, coverage, footprint, COG, and Zarr go by without quite landing, and most
tutorials assume the domain knowledge you're missing while carefully explaining
the code you already understand.

This series inverts that assumption. It spends its words on the **domain** (what these concepts *mean* and
*why they exist*) and trusts you with the code.
It is the on-ramp I wanted when I started doing EO work without the background.

## The guides

Each one is self-contained: it teaches the geospatial ideas it needs, so you can
start with whichever one matches the problem in front of you.

[**CoverageJSON Uncovered**](guides/coveragejson.qmd) is a complete, ground-up
guide to reading coverage data on the web, from your first document through the
edge cases.
